meta.title: Anxiety & Depression Therapy This Week | PhD/PsyD + Psychiatry | WTG
meta.description: Anxiety and depression therapy with a doctoral therapist this week. PhD/PsyD only with psychiatry on the same team, $280-300/session, OON help. Brooklyn, Austin, Miami, online.

hero.h1: Anxiety and depression therapy this week, with a PhD or PsyD therapist.
hero.subhead: Whether you're dealing with everyday low-grade overwhelm, panic that won't quit, depression that crept up over years, or OCD or trauma layered underneath, your first session can be this week. Every therapist holds a PhD or PsyD, and our board-certified psychiatrists are on the same team if medication ends up being part of the plan.
hero.trustLine: ✓ First session this week ✓ $280 to $380 per session ✓ Out-of-network claim filed for you
hero.imageAlt: Editorial portrait of a doctoral clinician in a WTG office

treat.h2: Anxiety and depression therapy is what we do, and the rest of the team is here when the work asks for more
treat.subhead: Six ways our doctoral team supports adults working through anxiety, depression, and what often comes alongside them. Every clinician PhD or PsyD.

cards.title: Anxiety therapy
cards.body: Evidence-based work for generalized anxiety, panic, social anxiety, health anxiety, and the everyday low-grade overwhelm that doesn't have a name.

cards.title: Depression therapy
cards.body: Doctoral support for major depression, persistent low mood, and the kind that crept up over years.

cards.title: OCD and trauma alongside
cards.body: Targeted work for OCD, PTSD, and trauma responses using methods proven for these specific conditions.

cards.title: Psychiatry on the same team
cards.body: Board-certified psychiatrists when medication can help. One care plan with your therapist, not two separate visits.

cards.title: Perinatal mental health
cards.body: Specialized doctoral care for pregnancy and postpartum depression and anxiety, on the same team.

cards.title: Couples and family work
cards.body: When anxiety or depression is reaching your partner or kids. Doctoral couples and family therapists on the same team.

comparison.h2: How anxiety and depression therapy at WTG compares to the rest of the field
comparison.subhead: What you'll get from most options versus what happens at WTG.

rows.them: A specialty center that matches WTG on credentials but has no integrated psychiatry. When you need medication, you start over with a new practice.
rows.us: Therapy and psychiatry on the same team, sharing notes with your consent. One care plan, not two.

rows.them: Master's-level therapists with weekend CBT certifications running anxiety and depression work.
rows.us: Every therapist holds a PhD or PsyD, with board-certified psychiatrists on the same team.

rows.them: You scroll a directory and pick a name based on a photo and a paragraph bio.
rows.us: A real coordinator picks the therapist for what you're working through, on a five-minute call. Match confirmed within 24 hours.

rows.them: Six-week waitlist before your first session.
rows.us: Most patients have their first session the same week they call.

rows.them: Out-of-network paperwork dumped on you to figure out.
rows.us: Reimbursify files your OON claim for you, and most plans pay back 50 to 80 percent of the session fee.

howItWorks.h2: Four steps from your first call to your first session
howItWorks.subhead: Under five minutes to start. No long forms, no chatbots, a real person answers and asks three questions about what you're working through.

steps.title: Call or tap to book
steps.body: Reach our patient coordinator by phone or online. A real person gets back to you the same day, on a five-minute call.

steps.title: Get matched in 24 hours
steps.body: We pair you with a doctoral therapist for what you're working through, your schedule, and the way you like to work. Match confirmed within 24 hours.

steps.title: First session
steps.body: Meet in person or over secure video, often within the same week.

steps.title: Make progress
steps.body: Concrete tools and check-ins so you can see the change in your day-to-day, not just in session.

whyWTG.h2: Five reasons adults choose WTG for anxiety and depression
whyWTG.subhead: Where doctoral training meets the moments medication may also matter.

bullets.title: Therapy and psychiatry on the same team
bullets.body: When medication can help, you're not bouncing between separate practices, retelling your story, or coordinating two records yourself. One team, one care plan, one set of notes.

bullets.title: Only doctoral-level therapists
bullets.body: Only PhD and PsyD clinicians for anxiety and depression. Most practices use master's-level therapists with weekend CBT certifications. We don't.

bullets.title: Real coordinator match in 24 hours
bullets.body: Not a quiz, not an algorithm. A person on our team listens to what you're working through and pairs you with the right doctoral therapist. Match confirmed within 24 hours.

bullets.title: Multi-method, not single-brand
bullets.body: Specialty centers bet everything on one method. Our doctoral therapists flex across CBT, DBT, interpersonal, and psychodynamic approaches based on what's actually in front of them.

bullets.title: Same therapist, every session
bullets.body: We match you once. You don't restart with someone new every time someone leaves the practice.

clinicians.eyebrow: Meet a few of our therapists
clinicians.h2: Doctoral specialists for anxiety and depression across Brooklyn, Austin, Miami, and online
clinicians.subhead: Our coordinator can pair you with any doctoral therapist on the team whose practice area fits what you're working through. The four below are placeholder slots for real WTG specialists; final names and headshots replace these before launch.

midCTA.body: Same-week first sessions for anxiety and depression therapy across Brooklyn, Austin, Miami, and online. The hardest part is making the call.

pricing.subhead: We're a cash-pay practice. No insurer dictating how many sessions you get, no diagnosis code filed to your permanent medical record.
pricing.reimbursement: Many patients have out-of-network benefits. We partner with Reimbursify, an independent service that submits your out-of-network claim to your insurer for you. Most plans pay back 50 to 80 percent of the session fee.
steps: We charge you the session fee at the time of service.
We send you a superbill you can submit to your insurance.
Reimbursify files the claim for you. You get reimbursed directly.

reviews.quote: I highly recommend Dr. Trubowitz, she's helped our family resolve some longstanding issues. This is the best group of doctors in Austin that I've worked with. The space is also beautiful and relaxing.

faq.h2: Everything adults ask before the first session

items.q: How much does therapy cost, and how long will it take?
items.a: Sessions are $280 to $380 each. Most adults meet weekly to start, often shifting to every other week as the work progresses. Patients doing focused work on anxiety or depression often see meaningful change in 12 to 20 sessions. Patients doing deeper work on OCD, trauma, or long-standing depression may stay longer. Your therapist will set goals with you in the first sessions and check progress regularly. We're not in the business of keeping you in therapy forever.

items.q: Do I need medication, or is therapy enough?
items.a: Many adults do well with therapy alone. Some benefit from medication alongside therapy. Some need a phase of medication first to stabilize before therapy can do its work. The right answer depends on what you're working through. Because we have psychiatrists on the same team as our therapists, you don't have to figure this out alone, your therapist and, if needed, your psychiatrist will recommend what fits. You can also start with therapy and add psychiatry later if it's needed.

items.q: What kind of therapy do you use for anxiety and depression?
items.a: Our doctoral therapists are trained in multiple evidence-based methods, including cognitive behavioral therapy, dialectical behavior therapy, interpersonal therapy, and psychodynamic approaches. Your coordinator pairs you with a therapist whose style fits how you like to work and what you're working through.

items.q: What if I've tried therapy before and it didn't work?
items.a: It happens, and it doesn't mean therapy can't work for you. Often the issue was the fit with the therapist, the modality used, or the timing in your life. Your coordinator can pair you with a doctoral clinician whose approach is different from what you tried last time. Tell your new therapist what didn't work before, we build from there.

items.q: Do you take my insurance for anxiety or depression therapy?
items.a: We're a cash-pay practice, so we don't bill insurance directly. Most patients use out-of-network benefits and get reimbursed 50 to 80 percent of the session fee. We give you a superbill and partner with Reimbursify, a claim-filing service, that submits it to your insurer for you.

form.eyebrow: Ready to take the next step?
form.h2: Tell us a little about what you're looking for
form.subhead: Our patient coordinator will call or email you back the same day. No waitlists, no chatbots, no pressure. The first session can be this week.
form.consent: By leaving your phone or email, you are consenting to receiving communications from us via text or email and that may include communications about your appointments, services, and marketing information related to our services. You may opt out of marketing information at any time. Your contact information will never be sold to any third party and will only be shared to the extent necessary to perform any services on your behalf.
form.phoneFallback: or call 512-675-1262 to speak with our coordinator now.
form.reassurance: Same-day response. No waitlists, no runaround.
